ambiguities.description: Member identity model (username, real name, profile, verification, pseudonymity)
Groups, spaces, or topics structure and how they are created and governed
Posting and content model (threads, replies, reactions, media, rich text)
Moderation and trust/safety model (pre-moderation, post-moderation, automated, community-driven)
Notification model (mentions, replies, digests, real-time, email, push)
Roles and permissions (member, moderator, admin, owner, custom roles)
Events or live interactions (webinars, AMAs, live chat, scheduled discussions)
Reputation, badges, or gamification if any (points, levels, achievements, leaderboards)
Privacy and publicness (public, private, invite-only, unlisted, searchable)
Reporting and analytics (engagement, growth, content health, moderation metrics)
Integrations with CRM, billing, support, or marketing systems
MVP community boundary — what features are in scope for first release

questions.question: What member identity model is required (real name, username, pseudonymous, verified)?
What moderation model is required (pre, post, automated, community-driven)?
Is the community public, private, or invite-only?
What content types must members be able to post?
Are events or live interactions required?
Must this integrate with CRM, billing, or support systems?

assumptions.assumption: Username-based identity with optional profile is acceptable for MVP
A simple topic-based structure is sufficient for MVP
Post-moderation with flagged content review is acceptable initially
Text posts with basic reactions are the core MVP content types

suggested_closures.decision: Community platform construction will start with username identity, topic-based spaces, post-moderation, and text posts under a configurable privacy model.
suggested_closures.rationale: A focused content and moderation model reduces initial risk while preserving principal control over community norms and access.

seed_tasks.title: Define member identity and verification model
seed_tasks.transformation: Document identity fields, verification requirements, and pseudonymity policy.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every identity field has a purpose and a privacy classification.

seed_tasks.title: Define group/space structure and governance
seed_tasks.transformation: Document how spaces are created, named, governed, and archived.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every space has an owner and moderation policy; no space is ungoverned.

seed_tasks.title: Define moderation and trust/safety policy
seed_tasks.transformation: Document moderation rules, escalation paths, automated filters, and appeal process.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every moderation action has a documented rule and an appeal path.

seed_tasks.title: Define posting and content model
seed_tasks.transformation: Document content types, formatting, media support, and posting rules.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every content type has a defined structure and moderation rule.

seed_tasks.title: Define privacy and access model
seed_tasks.transformation: Document public/private/invite settings and how they apply to spaces and content.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: Privacy and access model document exists.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every space has a clear visibility setting and access control.

seed_tasks.title: Define MVP community boundary
seed_tasks.transformation: Explicitly list features in MVP and what is out of scope.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: MVP scope document exists with explicit inclusions and exclusions.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Scope is achievable and every excluded feature is consciously deferred.

residuals.description: Member identity model is not defined.
Privacy and publicness model is not decided.
Posting and content model is not defined.
Events or live interactions strategy is undefined.
Reputation or gamification policy is not decided.
